title: Senior Doctors in England Vote for Potential Strikes
subtitle: Consultants seek better pay and working conditions, raising concerns about future NHS disruption.

summary.en: Consultant doctors in England have voted in favor of taking industrial action over pay and pension concerns. The British Medical Association (BMA) reports that 76% of those who voted support potential strikes. Health Secretary James Murray has criticized the decision, stating there is no justification for such action. While a mandate for strikes now exists, no specific dates have been announced yet. This development follows a recent agreement between the government and resident doctors to end their own long-running dispute.
summary.zh: 英格蘭的顧問醫師已投票贊成針對薪資與退休金問題採取勞工行動。英國醫學會指出，百分之七十六的投票者支持潛在的罷工。衛生大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞批評此決定，稱此類行動毫無正當理由。儘管目前已取得罷工授權，但尚未宣布具體日期。此發展發生在政府與住院醫師近期達成協議，結束長期爭議之後。

paragraphs.en: Consultant doctors across England have voted in favor of potential strike action, creating a new challenge for the National Health Service (NHS). In a recent ballot conducted by the British Medical Association (BMA), 76% of the senior doctors who participated said they would be willing to take industrial action. This vote gives the BMA a legal mandate—official permission from members—to organize strikes over the next 12 months.
paragraphs.zh: 英格蘭各地的顧問醫師已投票贊成採取潛在的罷工行動，這為國家醫療保健服務（NHS）帶來了新的挑戰。在英國醫學會（BMA）近期舉行的一項投票中，參與投票的資深醫師有百分之七十六表示願意採取勞工行動。這項投票賦予了英國醫學會法律授權，即獲得會員的正式許可，得以在未來十二個月內組織罷工。

paragraphs.en: Industrial action is a common term for strikes or other protests where workers stop or slow down their work to pressure employers for better conditions. For the NHS, this often means that many planned appointments and operations are cancelled or delayed. According to the BBC, hundreds of thousands of patient appointments have already been affected by various strikes over the past few years.
paragraphs.zh: 勞工行動是罷工或其他抗議活動的通用術語，指勞工停止或減緩工作，以向雇主施壓爭取更好的條件。對於國家醫療保健服務而言，這通常意味著許多預定的門診和手術將被取消或延期。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，過去幾年來，已有數十萬名病患的預約因各類罷工而受到影響。

paragraphs.en: This vote comes shortly after resident doctors, formerly known as junior doctors, reached a pay deal with the government to end their own three-year dispute. While the consultants have voted to strike, they have not yet announced specific dates for any walkouts. This delay offers a small window of hope that further talks between the union and the government could prevent the need for actual strikes.
paragraphs.zh: 此次投票是在住院醫師（先前稱為實習醫師）與政府達成薪資協議，結束長達三年爭議後不久進行的。儘管顧問醫師已投票支持罷工，但他們尚未宣布任何停工的具體日期。這段緩衝期提供了一絲希望，顯示工會與政府之間的進一步談判或許能避免實際罷工的發生。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Helen Neary and Dr. Shanu Datta, co-chairs of the BMA consultants committee, stated that the vote sends a clear message. They argued that consultants are no longer willing to accept what they describe as an attack on their pay and professional value. The BMA claims that the average consultant's pay is 26% lower in real terms than it was 17 years ago. Beyond pay, consultants are also asking for a shorter working week, moving from 40 hours down to 37.5 hours, and changes to how they are paid for working outside of normal hours.
paragraphs.zh: 英國醫學會顧問委員會共同主席海倫·尼里（Helen Neary）醫師與夏努·達塔（Shanu Datta）醫師表示，這項投票傳達了明確的訊息。他們主張，顧問醫師不再願意接受他們所稱的對薪資與專業價值的打擊。英國醫學會聲稱，顧問醫師的平均薪資實質價值比十七年前低了百分之二十六。除了薪資外，顧問醫師也要求縮短每週工時，從四十小時減至三十七點五小時，並要求調整非正常工作時間的薪資計算方式。

paragraphs.en: However, the government strongly disagrees with these claims. Health and Social Care Secretary James Murray stated that there is “no justification” for the strikes. He noted that consultants are among the top 2% of earners in the country, with an average salary of over £152,000 per year. According to The Guardian, Murray pointed out that starting pay for consultants has increased by 28.5% over the last four years. He urged the BMA to avoid a new cycle of disruptive action and instead work with the government to improve the lives of senior doctors.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，政府強烈反對這些說法。衛生與社會照護大臣詹姆斯·莫瑞（James Murray）表示，罷工「毫無正當理由」。他指出，顧問醫師屬於全國收入最高的前百分之二族群，平均年薪超過十五萬兩千英鎊。根據《衛報》報導，莫瑞指出顧問醫師的起薪在過去四年內已調升百分之二十八點五。他敦促英國醫學會避免陷入新一輪的破壞性行動，轉而與政府合作，改善資深醫師的生活條件。

paragraphs.en: The ballot results show a divided opinion within the profession. While 76% of those who voted supported the action, the total turnout was 51.5%. This is just above the 50% threshold required by law for a strike to be valid. Out of more than 35,000 eligible members, only 18,069 took part in the vote. Sources close to the Health Secretary have highlighted that this means fewer than two in five of all eligible consultants actually voted in favor of the strikes.
paragraphs.zh: 投票結果顯示該專業領域內部的意見分歧。雖然百分之七十六的投票者支持罷工，但總投票率僅為百分之五十點一五。這僅略高於法律規定罷工有效所需的百分之五十門檻。在超過三萬五千名具備資格的會員中，僅有一萬八千零六十九人參與投票。接近衛生大臣的消息人士強調，這意味著所有具備資格的顧問醫師中，實際投下贊成票的比例不到五分之二。

paragraphs.en: If the consultants do decide to walk out, it will create significant difficulties for hospital managers. While senior doctors would continue to provide emergency and urgent care, the cancellation of other services would be inevitable. The NHS has become more experienced at managing these situations by moving staff around to cover gaps, but a new wave of strikes would test these plans once again.
paragraphs.zh: 如果顧問醫師決定停工，將會為醫院管理層帶來重大困難。雖然資深醫師仍會提供緊急與急迫的醫療照護，但其他服務的取消將在所難免。國家醫療保健服務在應對此類情況方面已累積更多經驗，會透過調度人力來填補空缺，但新一波的罷工將再次考驗這些應變計畫。

paragraphs.en: For now, the situation remains uncertain. The BMA has made it clear that no strikes need to happen if the government is willing to address their concerns. As the government refuses to revisit the current pay offer, both sides are now in a difficult position. Patients and hospital staff will be watching closely to see if a solution can be found before any formal strike dates are announced.
paragraphs.zh: 目前局勢仍不明朗。英國醫學會已明確表示，若政府願意解決他們的疑慮，就不需要進行罷工。由於政府拒絕重新審視目前的薪資方案，雙方目前都處於艱難的處境。病患與醫院員工將密切關注，看在正式宣布罷工日期前是否能找到解決方案。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Value adjusted for inflation or changes in purchasing power, rather than just the raw currency amount.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 實質價值（扣除通貨膨脹等因素後的實際購買力）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為是「真實的條件」，但這裡指經濟上的實質價值。文中：The BMA claims that the average consultant's pay is 26% lower in real terms than it was 17 years ago.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Strikes where workers leave their place of work as a form of protest.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見動詞片語 walk out（離開），這裡作名詞使用，指罷工行動。文中：While the consultants have voted to strike, they have not yet announced specific dates for any walkouts.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The number of people who attend or participate in an event, especially an election or vote.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為是「轉向外面」，這裡指參與投票的人數比例。文中：While 76% of those who voted supported the action, the total turnout was 51.5%.

sources.name: BBC Health
sources.title: Consultants in England vote in favour of NHS strikes

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Consultant doctors in England vote for NHS strikes over pay and working week

quiz.question: What is the specific legal requirement mentioned regarding the consultant doctors' ballot?

options.A: At least 76% of all eligible members must vote in favor of the strike.
options.B: A minimum turnout of 50% of eligible members is required for the vote to be valid.
options.C: The BMA must announce strike dates at least 12 months in advance.
options.D: The government must officially approve the ballot results before a strike can occur.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第六段，法律規定投票率必須達到 50% 的門檻（threshold）才能使罷工投票有效。選項 A 錯誤，76% 是贊成票的比例，而非投票率門檻。選項 C 錯誤，文章提到的是授權有效期為 12 個月，而非提前 12 個月宣布日期。選項 D 錯誤，文章提到這是 BMA 獲得的法律授權，不需政府批准。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the government be confident that the strike mandate is weaker than the BMA suggests?

options.A: Because the government believes that consultants are already the highest-paid professionals in the country.
options.B: Because the BMA has failed to secure a pay deal for other types of doctors.
options.C: Because the low voter turnout means that a minority of the total eligible consultants actually voted 'yes'.
options.D: Because the government has already successfully negotiated with the resident doctors.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段指出，雖然有 76% 的投票者支持，但總投票率僅 51.5%，這意味著在所有合格的醫生中，只有不到五分之二的人實際投下贊成票。這支持了政府認為該授權缺乏廣泛共識的觀點。選項 A 與政府的立場有關但非投票數據本身。選項 B 與選項 D 雖然在文中提及，但並非政府用來質疑罷工授權強度的直接邏輯。

quiz.question: In the context of the article, what does the word 'real' mean in the phrase '26% lower in real terms'?

options.A: True and authentic, rather than fake.
options.B: Adjusted for inflation or purchasing power.
options.C: Physical and tangible, rather than digital.
options.D: Practical and realistic, rather than theoretical.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在經濟學語境中，「in real terms」意指經過通貨膨脹調整後的數值，用以反映實際購買力。選項 A 是指真實性，不符合經濟語境。選項 C 是指實體事物，與薪資購買力無關。選項 D 雖然在日常口語中常見，但在這裡描述薪資變動時，專指經過物價調整後的價值，而非單純的「務實」之意。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the current situation regarding the consultant doctors in England?

options.A: Consultant doctors have officially begun a series of walkouts to demand better working conditions.
options.B: The government and the BMA have reached a compromise to avoid further disruptions to the NHS.
options.C: Consultant doctors have voted to authorize strikes, but negotiations remain possible before any action is taken.
options.D: The NHS is planning to replace striking consultants with other medical staff to maintain hospital operations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章主旨圍繞在顧問醫生通過了罷工投票，但尚未宣布具體日期，且雙方仍有對話空間，這正是目前局勢的寫照。選項 A 錯誤，因為目前尚未開始罷工。選項 B 錯誤，雙方仍處於僵局，尚未達成妥協。選項 D 雖然提到 NHS 會調度人力，但這並非文章的核心主旨，且文章並未提到「取代」醫生的計畫。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
